post.description:
This API creates a PayPal payment and returns the PayPal approval URL.
The API:
1) reads logged-in workspace OW ID from header,
2) validates payment type/invoice details,
3) creates PayPal payment request, and
4) returns approval URL for user redirection.
Sends the payment receipt via email to the customer.

post.summary: Add or Update Third Party Cost
post.description: This API allows Workspace (WS) organization users to add or update third-party cost details. The API supports the following scenarios: 1) A WS user setting or updating the default third-party cost for their own workspace, and 2) A WS user setting or updating the third-party cost for an advertiser under the same workspace. If the WS user is setting or updating the cost for self, the owId can be passed as null and will be derived from the logged-in user context. For advertiser-level cost configuration, owId must be provided explicitly. The API validates user access and organization type before processing the request. If a third-party cost already exists for the given owId, the existing cost will be updated; otherwise, a new third-party cost record will be created. Upon successful add or update, the customer configuration is refreshed in Redis and an activity log is generated accordingly.| owId | Integer | OW ID of the workspace or advertiser for which the cost is being added or updated (optional for WS self, required for advertiser). || cost | Double | Third-party cost value to be added or updated (required, range >0 and <=999). |

Add or Update Third Party Cost for Adver.description: Add or Update Third Party Cost for Advertiser

Add or Update Default Third Party Cost f.description: Add or Update Default Third Party Cost for Workspace Self

200.description: Third-party cost added or updated successfully.

422.description: Invalid or inaccessible customer ow id passed.

patch.description: This API allows Workspace (WS) organization users to disable an existing third-party cost configuration. The API supports the following scenarios: 1) A WS user disabling the default third-party cost configured for their own workspace, and 2) A WS user disabling the third-party cost configured for an advertiser under the same workspace. The customerOwId path parameter represents the workspace or advertiser for which the cost needs to be disabled. The API validates user access, workspace ownership, and ensures that a third-party cost configuration exists for the given customer before proceeding. If a valid configuration is found, the third-party cost is marked as inactive and cost is set as null in the database. Upon successful disablement, the updated configuration is refreshed in Redis and an activity log entry is generated accordingly.| customerOwId | Integer | OW ID of the workspace or advertiser for which the third-party cost is being disabled (required). |

get.description:
Fetches insight fees details for a customer. If 'type' is provided, fetches for that specific report type; otherwise, fetches for all types.

**Available Categories and Verticals:**
- **vld** (Voter Level Data): Available for POLITICAL vertical
- **pld** (Publisher Level Data): Available for HEALTHCARE vertical
- **sls** (Script Lift Study): Available for HEALTHCARE vertical
- **aqs** (Ad Quality Suite): Available for HEALTHCARE vertical
- **ict** (ICT): Available for HEALTHCARE vertical
- **nld** (NLD): Available for both POLITICAL and HEALTHCARE verticals

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Insight report type (optional). Valid values: vld, pld, sls, aqs, ict, nld. If not provided, returns all applicable types for the customer's vertical.

patch.description: Update insight fees for a specific category (aqs, pld, vld, ict, nld) for a customer.
